steps.title: Track the sequence of bounces and impacts
steps.content:
Each time the ball hits the ground, it bounces up to exactly one-half the height from which it just fell. Let's track each impact and bounce:
  • Hit 1: Ball drops from 20 feet and hits the ground
  • Bounces to $\frac{1}{2} \times 20 = 10$ feet
  • Hit 2: Falls from 10 feet and hits the ground
  • Bounces to $\frac{1}{2} \times 10 = 5$ feet
  • Hit 3: Falls from 5 feet and hits the ground
  • Bounces to $\frac{1}{2} \times 5 = 2.5$ feet
  • Hit 4: Falls from 2.5 feet and hits the ground

steps.title: Identify the maximum height between hits 3 and 4
steps.content: Between the third and fourth time the ball hits the ground, it travels upward in a bounce after the third impact. The highest point it reaches is the peak of that bounce, which is $2.5$ feet. That matches choice C — C is correct.
